steps.explanation: The difference between angle A and angle B is $-\dfrac{5\pi}{12}$ radians (the choices confirm the radian measure is $-\dfrac{5\pi}{12}$, not $-5\pi$).
To convert radians to degrees, multiply by the factor $\dfrac{180^\circ}{\pi}$.

• A: $-\dfrac{5}{12} \cdot \dfrac{360^\circ}{\pi}$ — uses $\dfrac{360^\circ}{\pi}$ instead of $\dfrac{180^\circ}{\pi}$ and drops $\pi$ from the radian measure. Incorrect.
  • B: $-\dfrac{5}{12}\pi \cdot \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}$ — multiplies by $\dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}$, which converts degrees to radians (wrong direction). Incorrect.
  • C: $-\dfrac{5}{12}\pi \cdot \dfrac{180^\circ}{\pi}$ — correctly multiplies the radian measure by $\dfrac{180^\circ}{\pi}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: $-\dfrac{5}{12}\pi \cdot \dfrac{360^\circ}{\pi}$ — uses $\dfrac{360^\circ}{\pi}$, doubling the correct answer. Incorrect.
